---
title: "Softmax Regression Operation"
description: "Multi-class softmax regression classifier wrapping mlpack::SoftmaxRegression"
---

## Overview

`SoftmaxRegressionOperation` is a supervised multi-class classifier that wraps `mlpack::SoftmaxRegression<arma::mat>`. Unlike `LogisticRegressionOperation` (binary-only), it handles any number of classes $C \geq 2$ using L2-regularized softmax regression optimized with L-BFGS.

The operation produces calibrated per-class probability estimates via the softmax function, making it suitable as both a classifier and a dimensionality reduction technique that projects features into a $C$-dimensional probability simplex.

**Source files:**

- Header: `src/MLCore/models/supervised/SoftmaxRegressionOperation.hpp`
- Implementation: `src/MLCore/models/supervised/SoftmaxRegressionOperation.cpp`
- Tests: `tests/MLCore/SoftmaxRegressionOperation.test.cpp`

## Parameters

`SoftmaxRegressionParameters` in `MLModelParameters.hpp`:

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lambda` | `double` | 0.0001 | L2 regularization strength. Higher values penalize large weights more. |
| `max_iterations` | `std::size_t` | 10000 | Maximum L-BFGS optimizer iterations. |

## Architecture

### Pimpl Pattern

The implementation uses the pimpl idiom to isolate `mlpack.hpp` from consumers:

```
SoftmaxRegressionOperation.hpp  →  MLModelOperation (public API)
SoftmaxRegressionOperation.cpp  →  mlpack::SoftmaxRegression<arma::mat> (hidden)
```

The `Impl` struct holds:

- `std::unique_ptr<mlpack::SoftmaxRegression<arma::mat>>` — the trained model
- `num_classes`, `num_features` — cached training metadata
- `trained` — readiness flag

### Training

Training uses the constructor-based API:

```cpp
ens::L_BFGS optimizer;
optimizer.MaxIterations() = max_iterations;

model = std::make_unique<SRModel>(
    features, labels, num_classes, optimizer, lambda, false);
```

The number of classes is detected automatically from `arma::max(labels) + 1`.

### Prediction

- `predict()` calls `model->Classify(features, predictions)` for hard labels.
- `predictProbabilities()` calls `model->Classify(features, predictions, probabilities)` for a `(num_classes × num_observations)` matrix where each column sums to 1.0.

### Serialization

Uses cereal binary archives. The serialization format stores:

1. `num_classes` (size_t)
2. `num_features` (size_t)
3. The mlpack model (via mlpack's built-in cereal support)

## Registry Integration

Registered as `"Softmax Regression"` with `MLTaskType::MultiClassClassification` in `MLModelRegistry`.

```cpp
auto registry = MLModelRegistry{};
auto model = registry.create("Softmax Regression");
```

## Usage Example

```cpp
#include "MLCore/models/supervised/SoftmaxRegressionOperation.hpp"

MLCore::SoftmaxRegressionOperation sr;
MLCore::SoftmaxRegressionParameters params;
params.lambda = 0.001;
params.max_iterations = 5000;

// features: (D × N), labels: (1 × N) with values in [0, C)
sr.train(features, labels, &params);

// Hard predictions
arma::Row<std::size_t> predictions;
sr.predict(test_features, predictions);

// Probability estimates (C × N matrix, columns sum to 1)
arma::mat probs;
sr.predictProbabilities(test_features, probs);
```

## Comparison with LogisticRegression

| Feature | LogisticRegression | SoftmaxRegression |
|---|---|---|
| Classes | Binary only (0/1) | $C \geq 2$ |
| Task type | `BinaryClassification` | `MultiClassClassification` |
| Probabilities | Single probability per sample | $C$ probabilities per sample |
| Backend | `mlpack::LogisticRegression<arma::mat>` | `mlpack::SoftmaxRegression<arma::mat>` |
| Parameters | `lambda`, `step_size`, `max_iterations` | `lambda`, `max_iterations` |

## Test Coverage

11 test cases covering:

- Metadata (name, task type, default parameters)
- Multi-class training (3, 4, and 5 classes)
- Binary training via softmax (2 classes)
- Training error handling (empty features, empty labels, column mismatch, single class)
- Multi-class prediction accuracy
- Binary prediction accuracy via softmax
- Prediction error handling (untrained, empty features, dimension mismatch)
- Probability output (dimensions, sum-to-one, range, confidence)
- Save/load round-trip with prediction consistency
- Parameter variations (different lambda/iteration values)
- Registry integration (create via name)
